summary: High‑energy 1978 Providence show captured on a good‑quality audience recording, with smoking Eyes of the World, Let It Grow and Cassidy performances despite a few mic‑balance quirks.
review: The performance is widely praised for its fiery first‑set highlights (Half‑Step, Looks Like Rain) and an explosive second set featuring standout Eyes of the World, Let It Grow and Samson & Delilah. Reviewers note Jerry’s soaring guitar work and Phil’s incendiary intro to NFA, while the audience (AUD) recording is generally clear but suffers from a lopsided microphone mix and occasional drum length issues.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars, emphasizing strong musical moments; recording is good but not flawless, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and listenable AUD mix; two different microphone tracks cause a slight lopsided stereo effect and some reviewers note long drum sections detract slightly. No major drop‑outs, but mic balance could be improved.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds nicely with Half‑Step, LLR and Cassidy, while the second set maintains high energy through Samson & Delilah, Let It Grow and an incendiary Eyes of the World; overall pacing is praised as tight and uplifting.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice described as haggard but powerful; guitar tone sharp and lead‑centric with several soaring solos (Eyes, LLR, Let It Grow).
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s intro to NFA called “tears the intro” and his bass‑bombs add depth throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bass noted for colorful fills in Samson & Delilah and a big presence on Eyes of the World; solid rhythm foundation.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s broken‑chord placements and keyboard colors highlighted during Samson and NFA sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: Long drum passages mentioned as a minor detractor; two mic setup creates lopsided effect, with some reviewers preferring an AUD mix over SBD drums.
